seo.title: Building Code Generation with BeeAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with BeeAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: BeeAI enables robust Python-based AI agents for code generation with built-in memory, tools, and observability. Code generation use cases demand rigorous guardrails due to risks like malicious code injection, unbounded memory growth, and credential leakage. Vex integration provides runtime verification for safety, syntax, and sandbox execution.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with BeeAI
architectureOverview.description:
The BeeAI code generation agent follows a ReAct loop: LLM generates code based on user specs, executes in a sandbox, validates tests, and iterates. BeeAI's Agent class manages memory and tool calls, with custom tools for sandbox execution (docker-py), AST parsing, and test runners (pytest). State persistence uses JSON serialization, vulnerable to format inconsistencies from Granite models.

Key components include: CodeGenTool for LLM prompting, SandboxExecutor for isolated runs, TestValidator for pytest integration, and MemoryManager for session history. Vex listens to agent events (tool_start, tool_end, memory_update) to verify outputs against safety policies.

This architecture scales for iterative refinement but exposes risks in long sessions where memory balloons and tool inputs lack validation.

risks.name: Malicious Code Injection
risks.description: LLM generates exec() calls or system commands bypassing sandbox
risks.mitigation: Vex AST scanning + docker sandbox + network/FS restrictions

risks.name: Unbounded Memory Growth
risks.description: Long code refinement sessions accumulate massive conversation history
risks.mitigation: Vex memory size limits + summarization triggers

risks.name: Credential Leakage
risks.description: Generated code embeds API keys from agent memory or tools
risks.mitigation: Vex regex scanning + secret redaction policies

risks.name: Dependency Injection
risks.description: Malicious pip installs or unsafe package imports

implementation.title: BeeAI Agent with CodeGen Tools
implementation.description: Core agent setup with sandbox execution and test validation tools
implementation.code:
from beeai import Agent, Tool
from docker import DockerClient

class SandboxExecutor(Tool):
    name = 'sandbox_exec'
    description = 'Execute Python code in isolated Docker container'
    
    def execute(self, code: str) -> str:
        client = DockerClient(base_url='unix://var/run/docker.sock')
        container = client.containers.run(
            'python:3.11-slim',
            command=f'python -c """{code}"""',
            detach=True,
            remove=True,
            network_disabled=True,
            mem_limit='256m'
        )
        return container.logs().decode()

agent = Agent(
    model='granite-code-3b',
    tools=[SandboxExecutor()],
    memory_limit='10mb'
)

implementation.title: AST Syntax Validator Tool
implementation.description: Validates generated code syntax before sandbox execution

implementation.title: Test-Driven Refinement Loop
implementation.description: Iterative code improvement based on pytest failures
implementation.code:
class TestValidator(Tool):
    name = 'run_tests'
    
    def execute(self, code: str, tests: str) -> dict:
        # Write code/tests to temp files
        import tempfile, subprocess
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
            with open(f'{tmpdir}/code.py', 'w') as f:
                f.write(code)
            with open(f'{tmpdir}/test.py', 'w') as f:
                f.write(tests)
            
            result = subprocess.run(
                ['pytest', f'{tmpdir}/test.py', '-v'],
                capture_output=True, text=True
            )
            return {
                'passed': result.returncode == 0,
                'output': result.stdout,
                'errors': result.stderr
            }

implementation.title: Memory Management with Summarization
implementation.description: Prevent unbounded growth during long refinement sessions
implementation.code:
def memory_summarizer(memory):
    if len(str(memory)) > 10000:
        # Use LLM to summarize conversation
        summary_prompt = f'Summarize this agent session:\n{memory[-5000:]}'
        summary = agent.model.generate(summary_prompt)
        memory.clear()
        memory.append(summary)
    return memory

agent.add_event_listener('memory_update', memory_summarizer)

# Usage
response = agent.run('Write fibonacci function that passes these tests...')
agent.memory = memory_summarizer(agent.memory)

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Event Listener
vexIntegration.description: Capture all agent events for verification with high-risk policies

vexIntegration.title: Dependency Safety Verification
vexIntegration.description: Block malicious pip installs and unsafe imports

vexIntegration.title: Secret Redaction and Memory Limits
vexIntegration.description: Detect and redact credentials, enforce memory bounds
vexIntegration.code:
@vex.policy('memory-safety')
def verify_memory_state(event):
    memory = event.data.get('memory', '')
    
    # Secret detection
    secrets = vex.scan_secrets(memory)
    if secrets:
        event.data['memory'] = vex.redact(memory, secrets)
        vex.log_alert('Secrets detected and redacted')
    
    # Memory size limit
    if len(event.data['memory']) > 10_000_000:  # 10MB
        event.data['memory'] = summarize_memory(event.data['memory'])
        vex.log_metric('memory_truncated', size=len(event.data['memory']))
    
    return vex.approve(event.data)

agent.add_event_listener('memory_update', verify_memory_state)
